articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Invictus Energy Revives Farm-Out Talks Ahead of High-Stakes Zimbabwe Drilling Campaign
more.description: PERTH — Australia-listed Invictus Energy has reopened discussions with prospective partners over its onshore Zimbabwe assets, as it prepares to drill a potentially transformative exploration well in the Cabora Bassa Basin. The company confirmed it is engaging multiple interested parties in a renewed farm-out process, following the collapse of a previously anticipated investment agreement with [...] The post Invictus Energy Revives Farm-Out Talks Ahead of High-Stakes Zimbabwe Drilling Campaign appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Chivayo lashes out at Coltart over criticism of ZITF Hulking appearance with presidents
more.description: BULAWAYO – Tender magnate Wicknell Chivayo fired back at Bulawayo mayor David Coltart on Monday after the opposition politician questioned his presence alongside President Emmerson Mnangagwa and visiting Botswana President Duma Boko during a tour of exhibition stands at the Zimbabwe International Trade Fair (ZITF) in Bulawayo last week. more.title: As Mnangagwa clings on, Chiwenga tells parable of the king who wouldn’t let go
more.description: HARARE — Vice President Constantino Chiwenga delivered what appeared, on the surface, to be a spiritual address at a Roman Catholic Church event in Murewa on Saturday, but his choice of scripture carried unmistakable resonance against the backdrop of President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s push to rewrite Zimbabwe’s constitution and cling to power. Extolling the virtues of [...] The post As Mnangagwa clings on, Chiwenga tells parable of the king who wouldn’t let go appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.description: President Mnangagwa Orders Removal of SADC Trade Barriers to Boost Regional Trade President Emmerson Mnangagwa has ordered an immediate push to eliminate trade restrictions as Government ramps up efforts to improve cross-border commerce and deepen economic integration.
